reason_for_decision: The article passes because it turns data-center perimeter planning into a clear three-zone model tied to geometry, sensor roles, and operator workflow.
strengths: The fence-line, roofline, and airspace framing is easy to understand and gives the article a strong deployment-specific identity.
The piece keeps the zoning model connected to real operator questions, escalation logic, and verification needs instead of leaving it as a purely conceptual site map.
It provides clear practical value for data-center security planning by tying zone definitions to sensor placement and blind-spot analysis.
summary_assessment: This is a strong deployment article with a clear audience and solid planning value. It does a good job of turning layered-security principles into a site-specific zoning method.
